projects.name: Project Phoenix
projects.description: Next-generation customer analytics platform with real-time event processing.

projects.name: Redis v7 Migration
projects.description: Upgrade production caching layer from Redis 6.2 to Redis 7.x.

tickets.title: Deploy new caching layer for Phoenix
tickets.description: Implement Redis-backed session cache and event deduplication for Phoenix API. Blocked until Redis cluster stability is restored.

tickets.title: Redis cluster instability — memory eviction storms
tickets.description: Production Redis cluster experiencing repeated OOM evictions and elevated latency (p99 > 450ms). Suspected misconfigured maxmemory-policy after partial upgrade.

tickets.title: Validate Redis persistence settings for Phoenix workloads
tickets.description: Review AOF/RDB settings and failover behavior for Phoenix high-write event streams.

tickets.title: Phoenix API integration tests against staging cache
tickets.description: End-to-end tests for Phoenix event pipeline. Cannot proceed until PROJ-101 caching layer is deployed.

incidents.title: Redis cluster degraded — elevated latency and eviction storms
incidents.summary: At 03:42 UTC, monitoring alerted on Redis p99 latency spike and key eviction rate 12x baseline. Phoenix staging deployments paused. Root cause under investigation — possible version mismatch between primary and replica nodes.

timeline.event: SEV-2 declared — Redis p99 latency spike detected
Key eviction rate reached 12x baseline; Phoenix staging deployments paused
OPS-402 opened; David assigned as incident commander
Root cause identified: Redis 6.2/7.0 version skew on replica nodes

documents.title: Project Phoenix Architecture Overview
documents.content: Project Phoenix requires Redis v7 for stream processing and client-side caching. The platform depends on sub-50ms cache reads for real-time analytics dashboards. Launch is contingent on Redis v7 migration completing in production.

documents.title: Redis Production Runbook
documents.content: Production Redis cluster runs Redis 6.2.14 across 3 primary + 3 replica nodes. Maxmemory-policy must be allkeys-lru. Any version skew between nodes triggers automatic failover — documented SEV-2 procedure applies.

documents.title: INC-2024-089 — Initial Findings (Draft)
documents.content: Preliminary analysis: one replica node was upgraded to Redis 7.0 during a maintenance window while primaries remain on 6.2.14. Replication protocol incompatibility caused intermittent sync failures and memory pressure. Phoenix PROJ-101 deployment blocked until cluster is homogenized.

documents.title: Redis Labs Enterprise SLA
documents.content: Enterprise support guarantees 1-hour response for SEV-2 incidents. Vendor recommends homogeneous Redis 7.x across all nodes before enabling Phoenix workload patterns.
